title: Dilution Calculator: Plan Serial Dilutions and Back-Calculate Titers from Plate Count Data
author: Abedon, Stephen
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Dilution Calculator is a free, browser-based tool for planning serial dilution series and back-calculating phage or bacterial titers from plate count data. The Dilution Planner tab takes a starting titer and a target — either a final titer in a tube or a target plaque/colony count on a plate — and generates a complete step-by-step dilution protocol. For the "reduce liquid titer" mode, whole-number serial steps (100-fold and/or 10-fold) are followed by a final fractional transfer step that hits the target concentration exactly. For the "deliver to a plate" mode, the calculator identifies the exact plating volume needed to achieve a target count, and provides a table of power-of-ten plating volume brackets with expected counts and countability comments. The Titer from Plate Count tab back-calculates original sample titers from plaque or colony counts, accepting either a single total dilution factor or individually listed dilution steps. Three educational tabs cover exponents, scientific notation, and significant figures, each with interactive calculators. The tool is aimed at microbiology students and phage researchers alike, and is part of the phage.org calculator suite.
